plssem 0.1.1
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Categorical Models with plssem
    • Higher Order Models
    • Interaction Models with plssem
    • Linear Models with plssem
    • Handling Missing Values with plssem
    • Multilevel Models with plssem
    • Ordinal Regression with plssem
    • Parallel Bootstrapping with plssem

Multilevel Models with plssem

Source: vignettes/multilevel-models.Rmd
multilevel-models.Rmd

This vignette shows examples of multilevel random slopes and intercept models, with both continuous and ordinal data.

Random Slopes Model 

slopes_model <- "
  X =~ x1 + x2 + x3
  Z =~ z1 + z2 + z3
  Y =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  W =~ w1 + w2 + w3
  Y ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
  W ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
"

Continuous Indicators 

fit_slopes_cont <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopes,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_slopes_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   127.210
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                74
#>   SRMR                                           0.008
#>   RMSEA                                          0.012
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.860
#>   x2                                             0.683
#>   x3                                             0.772
#>   z1                                             0.839
#>   z2                                             0.694
#>   z3                                             0.759
#>   y1                                             0.838
#>   y2                                             0.726
#>   y3                                             0.750
#>   w1                                             0.840
#>   w2                                             0.695
#>   w3                                             0.771
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.542
#>   W                                              0.553
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.927      0.010   90.744    0.000
#>     x2              0.826      0.012   66.701    0.000
#>     x3              0.879      0.008  108.510    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.916      0.011   84.919    0.000
#>     z2              0.833      0.011   74.592    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.010   84.010    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.915      0.012   77.541    0.000
#>     y2              0.852      0.014   62.985    0.000
#>     y3              0.866      0.017   52.192    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.916      0.012   76.579    0.000
#>     w2              0.834      0.018   47.569    0.000
#>     w3              0.878      0.014   63.886    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.293      0.023   12.611    0.000
#>     Z               0.444      0.040   11.181    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.393      0.033   11.960    0.000
#>     Z               0.248      0.055    4.511    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.010      0.005    1.772    0.076
#>    .W               0.010      0.007    1.393    0.164
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.175      0.012   14.648    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.004      0.006   -0.574    0.566
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.025      0.017   -1.495    0.135
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.008    1.608    0.108
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.003      0.011    0.277    0.782
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.009      0.014    0.645    0.519
#>     W~X             0.013      0.014    0.969    0.332
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.458      0.040   11.434    0.000
#>    .W               0.447      0.030   15.039    0.000
#>    .x1              0.140      0.019    7.386    0.000
#>    .x2              0.317      0.020   15.503    0.000
#>    .x3              0.228      0.014   16.001    0.000
#>    .z1              0.161      0.020    8.117    0.000
#>    .z2              0.306      0.019   16.376    0.000
#>    .z3              0.241      0.018   13.341    0.000
#>    .y1              0.162      0.021    7.554    0.000
#>    .y2              0.274      0.023   11.970    0.000
#>    .y3              0.250      0.028    8.777    0.000
#>    .w1              0.160      0.022    7.294    0.000
#>    .w2              0.305      0.029   10.478    0.000
#>    .w3              0.229      0.024    9.542    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.086      0.020    4.281    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.806    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.105      0.020    5.137    0.000
#>     W~1             0.059      0.014    4.353    0.000
#>     W~X             0.095      0.016    5.809    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.144      0.030    4.873    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_slopes_ord <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopesOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomSlopesOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_slopes_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   263.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                74
#>   SRMR                                           0.010
#>   RMSEA                                          0.023
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.870
#>   x2                                             0.669
#>   x3                                             0.789
#>   z1                                             0.841
#>   z2                                             0.714
#>   z3                                             0.758
#>   y1                                             0.844
#>   y2                                             0.711
#>   y3                                             0.754
#>   w1                                             0.835
#>   w2                                             0.681
#>   w3                                             0.785
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.539
#>   W                                              0.550
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.933      0.012   75.012    0.000
#>     x2              0.818      0.011   73.303    0.000
#>     x3              0.888      0.012   73.193    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.917      0.011   81.010    0.000
#>     z2              0.845      0.012   72.747    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.011   78.235    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.918      0.011   83.222    0.000
#>     y2              0.843      0.014   60.762    0.000
#>     y3              0.869      0.015   58.331    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.914      0.010   87.677    0.000
#>     w2              0.825      0.014   60.340    0.000
#>     w3              0.886      0.016   54.200    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.290      0.020   14.384    0.000
#>     Z               0.451      0.034   13.275    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.391      0.032   12.268    0.000
#>     Z               0.245      0.050    4.875    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.011      0.006    2.003    0.045
#>    .W               0.009      0.008    1.116    0.265
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.168      0.011   15.680    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.007      0.005   -1.334    0.182
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.025      0.012   -2.114    0.035
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.008    1.487    0.137
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.004      0.010    0.380    0.704
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.010      0.011    0.844    0.399
#>     W~X             0.016      0.011    1.456    0.145
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.461      0.026   17.586    0.000
#>    .W               0.450      0.030   14.768    0.000
#>    .x1              0.130      0.023    5.631    0.000
#>    .x2              0.331      0.018   18.125    0.000
#>    .x3              0.211      0.022    9.741    0.000
#>    .z1              0.159      0.021    7.633    0.000
#>    .z2              0.286      0.020   14.519    0.000
#>    .z3              0.242      0.019   12.547    0.000
#>    .y1              0.156      0.020    7.696    0.000
#>    .y2              0.289      0.023   12.410    0.000
#>    .y3              0.246      0.026    9.522    0.000
#>    .w1              0.165      0.019    8.752    0.000
#>    .w2              0.319      0.023   14.119    0.000
#>    .w3              0.215      0.029    7.459    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.084      0.014    5.941    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.816    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.101      0.012    8.473    0.000
#>     W~1             0.058      0.012    4.663    0.000
#>     W~X             0.099      0.015    6.630    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.142      0.022    6.499    0.000

Random Intercepts Model 

intercepts_model <- '
  f =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  f ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + w1 + w2 + (1 | cluster)
'

Continuous Indicators 

fit_intercepts_cont <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomIntercepts,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_intercepts_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    24.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                31
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.000
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.891
#>   y2                                             0.785
#>   y3                                             0.814
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.744
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.944      0.008  113.037    0.000
#>     y2              0.886      0.010   90.051    0.000
#>     y3              0.902      0.009  106.123    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.238      0.007   32.915    0.000
#>     x2              0.162      0.007   24.656    0.000
#>     x3              0.077      0.006   13.386    0.000
#>     w1              0.128      0.045    2.855    0.004
#>     w2              0.091      0.038    2.377    0.017
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.013      0.010   -1.302    0.193
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.104      0.011    9.021    0.000
#>     x3              0.004      0.013    0.326    0.745
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.097      0.010    9.676    0.000
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.041      0.045   -0.908    0.364
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.256      0.018   13.877    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.109      0.016    6.881    0.000
#>    .y2              0.215      0.017   12.263    0.000
#>    .y3              0.186      0.015   12.162    0.000
#>     f~1             0.621      0.024   26.136    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_intercepts_ord <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomInterceptsOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomInterceptsOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_intercepts_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    25.508
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                31
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.000
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.885
#>   y2                                             0.788
#>   y3                                             0.809
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.723
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.941      0.013   75.050    0.000
#>     y2              0.888      0.015   60.259    0.000
#>     y3              0.899      0.012   72.484    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.241      0.008   31.356    0.000
#>     x2              0.158      0.009   18.397    0.000
#>     x3              0.080      0.006   13.734    0.000
#>     w1              0.121      0.049    2.466    0.014
#>     w2              0.081      0.045    1.819    0.069
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.012      0.009   -1.343    0.179
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.110      0.010   10.778    0.000
#>     x3              0.012      0.012    1.019    0.308
#>     w1              0.001      0.003    0.240    0.810
#>     w2              0.001      0.004    0.268    0.789
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.099      0.012    8.500    0.000
#>     w1             -0.003      0.003   -1.183    0.237
#>     w2             -0.001      0.003   -0.440    0.660
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1             -0.003      0.002   -1.268    0.205
#>     w2              0.003      0.003    1.163    0.245
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.025      0.047   -0.531    0.596
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.277      0.015   18.931    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.115      0.024    4.840    0.000
#>    .y2              0.212      0.026    8.087    0.000
#>    .y3              0.191      0.022    8.588    0.000
#>     f~1             0.601      0.023   26.379    0.000
